stem: A ISP analysis study measures the download speed (Mbps) for a sample of 20 subscribers. The sample mean is 158.8 Mbps with a sample standard deviation of 16.3 Mbps. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 158.8 Mbps. 95% CI = [151.2, 166.4]. Point estimate: 158.8 Mbps.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
